items.title: Man Utd handed Tchouameni boost - Friday's gossip
items.summary: Real Madrid open to selling Aurelien Tchouameni to Manchester United, Arsenal eye John Stones and Ezri Konsa, Chelsea favourites for Kerim Alajbegovic, Yan Diomande back on radar of Premier League clubs and Daizen Maeda set for Ipswich Town move.

items.title: Real Madrid willing to sell Aurelien Tchouameni to Man Utd in £68m deal to fund ambitious Rodri transfer
items.summary: JOSE MOURINHO is ready to sell Aurelien Tchouameni to Manchester United so Real Madrid can fund a swoop for World Cup winner Rodri. The Red Devils have enquired about £68million-rated Tchouameni, 26, with boss Michael Carrick keen on a specialist defensive midfielder to join new boys Andrey Santos a

items.title: Premier League star immediately gifts grandad World Cup winner’s medal in touching gesture after incredible sacrifice
items.summary: PEDRO PORRO fulfilled a lifelong promise by handing his World Cup winner's medal straight to the man who helped make it all possible. The Tottenham defender returned home to Spain after lifting football's biggest prize – before placing the gold medal around the neck of his proud grandfather Antonio.

items.title: Arsenal and Tottenham set to go head to head for £51m-rated World Cup star, 21, who faced England
items.summary: ARSENAL and Tottenham are preparing to go head-to-head in a fierce transfer battle for RB Leipzig winger Antonio Nusa. items.title: Papers: Valuations of several Man Utd midfield targets revealed
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Friday's newspapers...

items.title: Pep Guardiola ‘makes decision’ on Italy job with Man City legend ‘demanding £17MILLION-a-year salary’
items.summary: PEP GUARDIOLA is reportedly ready to snub the chance to become Italy's new boss. The six-time Premier League title winner, 55, has been assessing his options after quitting Manchester City at the end of last season. items.title: Chelsea played a blinder beating Arsenal to £117m Morgan Rogers but Blues are gaslighting fans over three major issues
items.summary: ON PAPER there are plenty of reasons for Chelsea fans to be cheerful. A shiny new manager, a big-money signing and - having played a blinder by losing on the last day of the season - no Conference League distraction. items.title: 'Good luck to Dirk...': Littler dazzles in Blackpool as 'frightening' form continues
items.summary: If a player hit a nine-darter on the World Matchplay stage, that would usually go down as their highlight. That is just the tip of the iceberg for Luke Littler in 2026.

items.title: Murray returns as Draper's coach for Mubadala DC Open
items.summary: Andy Murray and Jack Draper appear to be working together once again after the 24-year-old was forced to miss out on Wimbledon earlier this month.
